title: Werauhia montana
taxonomy: Werauhia montana is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae, commonly known as pineapples or air plants. This plant is native to South America, specifically the Andean regions. Werauhia montana is classified in the genus Werauhia, which is a group of epiphytic plants. The plant has been reclassified from the genus Pitcairnia to Werauhia in recent years due to advances in taxonomic research. Werauhia montana is part of the Bromeliad family, which comprises over 3,000 species of plants.

intro: Werauhia montana, also known as the mountain bromeliad, is a species of epiphytic plant native to the cloud forests of South America. The health benefits of Werauhia montana include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and wounds. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
